title: The Rise of 'AI Washing': Why Companies Are Pretending to Use Artificial Intelligence
subtitle: Businesses are rushing to rebrand as AI experts, but experts warn that much of the hype is just simple automation.

summary.en: Many companies are now calling their products 'AI-powered' to attract attention and investment. Public relations experts say this trend, known as 'AI washing,' often misleads the public about what technology actually does. While some businesses use real artificial intelligence, others are simply rebranding old automation tools. Critics argue that this constant hype makes people tired of hearing about the technology. Meanwhile, some writers and coders worry that relying too much on AI tools stops people from doing the hard work of thinking.
summary.zh: 許多公司現在將其產品稱為「人工智慧驅動」以吸引注意力和投資。公關專家表示，這種被稱為「人工智慧漂綠」的趨勢，往往會誤導大眾對於科技實際功能的認知。雖然有些企業確實使用真正的人工智慧，但其他企業只是將舊有的自動化工具重新包裝。批評者認為，這種持續的炒作讓大眾對相關科技感到厭倦。同時，一些作家和程式設計師擔心，過度依賴人工智慧工具會使人們停止進行艱難的思考工作。

paragraphs.en: In the modern business world, artificial intelligence (AI) has become the most popular buzzword. From shoe brands to basketball equipment companies, businesses everywhere are trying to prove they are part of the AI revolution. However, public relations professionals say that many of these companies are going too far. They are now using a term called “AI washing” to describe businesses that claim to be AI experts when they are actually just using basic, older technology.
paragraphs.zh: 在現代商業世界中，人工智慧（AI）已成為最熱門的流行語。從鞋類品牌到籃球設備公司，各行各業的企業都試圖證明自己參與了這場人工智慧革命。然而，公關專業人士指出，許多公司做得太過火了。他們現在使用「人工智慧漂綠」（AI washing）這個詞，來形容那些明明只是使用基礎舊技術，卻自稱是人工智慧專家的企業。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, communications experts are feeling tired of this trend. They often have to tell their bosses that simply adding the word “AI” to a product name will not impress journalists. One publicist based in London noted that reporters often roll their eyes when they hear the term because they see so many false claims. Companies are desperate to stay relevant, but their attempts to link their products to AI are often very weak. For example, one property company tried to market a simple handheld building scanner as an AI tool. While the device might use a small amount of software to speed up the work, it is really just a form of automation—a process where machines perform simple, repeated tasks—rather than true artificial intelligence.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，傳播專家對這種趨勢感到厭倦。他們經常必須告訴老闆，單純在產品名稱中加入「人工智慧」這幾個字，並不會讓記者留下深刻印象。一位駐倫敦的公關人員提到，記者們聽到這個詞時常會翻白眼，因為他們看過太多不實的宣稱。企業極力想維持市場相關性，但他們將產品與人工智慧連結的嘗試往往非常薄弱。例如，一家房地產公司曾試圖將一款簡單的手持式建築掃描器行銷為人工智慧工具。雖然該裝置可能使用少量的軟體來加速工作，但它實際上只是一種自動化形式——即機器執行簡單、重複性任務的過程——而非真正的人工智慧。

paragraphs.en: This “AI washing” is happening across many different industries. The Guardian reports that some genetics companies have hyped up blood tests as being powered by AI, while other brands have tried to label everything from basketball hoops to safety lasers with the same term. Imran Ariff, a media strategist, explained that brands can easily “drink their own Kool-Aid.” This means they become so proud of their work that they start to believe their own exaggerated marketing, even when it is not true. These companies often force their public relations teams to send out press releases about AI, even when the PR workers know that the claims are not honest.
paragraphs.zh: 這種「人工智慧漂綠」現象正發生在許多不同的產業中。據《衛報》報導，一些基因檢測公司大肆宣傳其血液檢測是由人工智慧驅動，而其他品牌則試圖將從籃球框到安全雷射等所有產品都貼上同樣的標籤。媒體策略師伊姆蘭·阿里夫（Imran Ariff）解釋說，品牌很容易「自我催眠」（drink their own Kool-Aid）。這意味著他們對自己的工作感到過度自豪，以至於開始相信自己誇大的行銷說詞，即便事實並非如此。這些公司經常強迫公關團隊發布關於人工智慧的新聞稿，即便公關人員心知肚明這些宣稱並不誠實。

paragraphs.en: There are several reasons why companies are doing this. Many large businesses are currently cutting thousands of jobs while claiming they are moving toward AI. By branding themselves as tech-focused, they hope to look like modern, forward-thinking organizations. However, this strategy has risks. As one account director noted, the public is becoming “numb” to the constant use of words like “AI-driven” or “AI-powered.” When every product is called AI, the term loses its meaning. Journalists are now much less likely to cover these stories because they have seen so many examples of companies exaggerating their capabilities.
paragraphs.zh: 企業這麼做有幾個原因。許多大型企業目前正在裁減數千名員工，同時宣稱他們正轉向人工智慧。透過將自己定位為科技導向，他們希望看起來像是現代化、具前瞻性的組織。然而，這種策略存在風險。正如一位客戶總監所指出的，大眾對於「人工智慧驅動」或「人工智慧支援」這類詞彙的頻繁使用已經感到「麻木」。當每個產品都被稱為人工智慧時，這個詞就失去了意義。記者現在不太願意報導這些故事，因為他們已經看過太多公司誇大其詞的案例。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the marketing hype, some experts are concerned about how society uses these tools. In a recent article for The Guardian, writer Wendy Liu expressed worry about the way AI is changing human habits. She argues that tasks like coding and writing are valuable because they are difficult. When people use AI to do this work for them, they avoid the “hard thinking” that helps them learn and grow. She calls this “cognitive offloading,” which is the act of letting a machine do the thinking so that a person does not have to. She fears that if young people see technology as a “black box”—a system where we see the result but cannot understand how it works—they will lose control over their own creative processes.
paragraphs.zh: 除了行銷炒作之外，一些專家也擔心社會使用這些工具的方式。在《衛報》最近的一篇文章中，作家溫蒂·劉（Wendy Liu）表達了對人工智慧改變人類習慣的擔憂。她主張，編碼和寫作等任務之所以有價值，是因為它們具有難度。當人們使用人工智慧代勞時，他們就避開了有助於學習與成長的「深度思考」。她稱此為「認知卸載」（cognitive offloading），即讓機器代為思考，使人不必動腦。她擔心，如果年輕人將科技視為一個「黑盒子」——即一種我們只見結果，卻無法理解其運作原理的系統——他們將會失去對自身創作過程的掌控。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, the rush to label everything as AI reflects a wider pressure on businesses to appear cutting-edge. While some companies are genuinely using advanced technology to solve complex problems, many others are simply trying to follow a trend. For the average consumer, it is becoming harder to tell the difference between real innovation and simple marketing tricks. As the hype continues, the challenge for the public will be to look past the labels and understand what these tools are actually doing. For now, it seems that “AI washing” will remain a common, if frustrating, part of the business landscape.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根究柢，急於將所有事物貼上人工智慧標籤，反映出企業面臨的一種更廣泛的壓力，即必須展現出尖端科技感。雖然有些公司確實正在利用先進技術解決複雜問題，但許多其他公司只是試圖跟風。對於一般消費者而言，要分辨真正的創新與單純的行銷噱頭變得越來越困難。隨著炒作持續，大眾面臨的挑戰將是看穿這些標籤，並理解這些工具實際上的用途。目前看來，「人工智慧漂綠」仍將是商業環境中一個常見且令人沮喪的部分。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To move one's eyes upward as a way of showing annoyance, disbelief, or that one thinks something is silly.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 翻白眼（表示不屑、不耐煩或覺得對方很蠢）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個常見的肢體語言慣用語，用來表達對某事的不以為然。文中：One publicist based in London noted that reporters often roll their eyes when they hear the term because they see so many false claims.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become so convinced of one's own marketing or ideas that one loses touch with reality.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 對自己的行銷話術或觀點深信不疑，以至於脫離現實。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個源自美國文化的隱喻，指企業過度沉浸在自己的宣傳中。文中：Imran Ariff, a media strategist, explained that brands can easily “drink their own Kool-Aid.”

keyTerms.definitionEn: Unable to feel or react to something because of overexposure.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於描述身體部位失去知覺，這裡指對行銷用語感到疲乏、無感。文中：As one account director noted, the public is becoming “numb” to the constant use of words like “AI-driven” or “AI-powered.”

keyTerms.definitionEn: To exaggerate the importance or quality of something to create excitement.
keyTerms.noteZh: 由 hype（炒作）演變而來的片語，常用於行銷語境。文中：The Guardian reports that some genetics companies have hyped up blood tests as being powered by AI, while other brands have tried to label everything from basketball hoops to safety lasers with the same term.

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: ‘AI washing’: firms are scrambling to rebrand themselves as tech-focused

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: I avoid AI tools because thinking is supposed to be hard. It’s what makes us human | Wendy Liu

quiz.question: According to the article, why did a property company's marketing of a building scanner fail to impress experts?

options.A: The device was too expensive for the average consumer.
options.B: The device relied on basic automation rather than true artificial intelligence.
options.C: The company failed to release a press release to explain the technology.
options.D: The scanner was already an outdated tool that had been replaced by newer models.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，該房地產公司將手持掃描器行銷為 AI 工具，但文中明確指出該設備僅是「自動化（automation）」而非真正的「人工智慧（AI）」。選項 A 文章未提及價格；選項 C 與文章提到的公關人員發送新聞稿的事實不符；選項 D 雖然提到技術較舊，但重點在於它被錯誤標籤為 AI，而非被取代。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between journalists and companies currently using the term 'AI'?

options.A: Journalists are eager to report on new AI developments to help companies grow.
options.B: Journalists have developed a skeptical attitude due to the prevalence of exaggerated claims.
options.C: Companies are successfully using the term 'AI' to increase their media coverage.
options.D: Journalists are now working closely with PR teams to verify AI claims before publication.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第二段與第四段，記者對於過度使用「AI」一詞感到厭煩，甚至會翻白眼，且因為看到太多誇大的行銷宣傳，現在更不願意報導這類故事。選項 A 與文章描述的記者態度相反；選項 C 錯誤，因為文章指出這種策略已逐漸失效；選項 D 文章中並未提到記者與公關團隊合作查核，反而提到記者對這些宣傳持懷疑態度。

quiz.question: In the third paragraph, what does the phrase 'drink their own Kool-Aid' mean in the context of business marketing?

options.A: To become so convinced by one's own marketing lies that one believes they are true.
options.B: To consume expensive products to show loyalty to the company brand.
options.C: To share secret information with competitors to gain a market advantage.
options.D: To ignore the advice of public relations experts in order to save money.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中提到「drink their own Kool-Aid」後隨即解釋，這意味著品牌對自己的工作過於自豪，以至於開始相信那些並不真實的誇大行銷。這是一個慣用語，指人被自己的說法洗腦。選項 B、C、D 均與文中提供的定義完全無關，屬於對該慣用語的錯誤猜測。

quiz.question: What is the primary message the author conveys regarding 'AI washing'?

options.A: AI is a dangerous technology that should be banned from all consumer products.
options.B: Public relations professionals are the main cause of the decline in modern technology.
options.C: The term 'AI' is being misused by many companies to appear modern, which creates confusion for consumers.
options.D: Companies should stop trying to innovate and focus only on basic automation tools.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文的核心在於探討企業為了追求時髦而濫用「AI」標籤（AI washing），導致大眾難以分辨真正的創新與行銷手段。選項 A 過度推論，文章並未主張禁止 AI；選項 B 將責任歸咎於公關人員不公平，文中指出這是企業為了跟風的策略；選項 D 並非文章主旨，文章並未反對創新，而是反對虛假的行銷標籤。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
